title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
summary: Ryo Lu discusses how AI tools like Cursor change the development loop and the risk of black-box abstraction in building software. He argues that staying close to the material, understanding the underlying systems, is essential to maintaining craft and preventing software from becoming a disconnected output.
writeup: Ryo Lu argues that AI tools such as Cursor reshape the development loop and introduce a risk of black-box abstraction, where software becomes a disconnected output rather than an understood system. The talk holds that staying close to the material, meaning understanding the underlying systems, is essential to maintaining craft as code generation and AI-assisted development expand. The framing connects this to everyday work in prototyping and debugging.
